author: Tester <t@t.com>
body:
# Findings

Landed `migrate_v2_v3` module + `Storage::maybe_migrate_v2_to_v3`
wired into `Storage::open` (after v1→v2). For each issue id on
`bookmarks(issues)`, walks the v2 op chain via `jj log` (path-
filtered on both v2 and v1 paths), and re-lands each commit on
`refs/jjf/issues/<id>`: per-commit tree carries the byte-
identical `issue.json` / `comments.jsonl` blobs read from the v2
commit, message preserved verbatim (trailer block round-trips).
Memories follow the same shape on `refs/jjf/memories/<key>`.

Bookmark deletion + sentinel-plant happen after every issue +
memory ref is built; sentinel is the idempotency marker. Per-
issue idempotency: a ref that already exists is skipped. Six
integration tests in `tests/v2_to_v3_migration.rs` cover the
acceptance grid (round-trip records/comments, chain-length
preservation, no-op re-open, memories carry, v1→v2→v3 chained,
partial-migration skip).

Auto-migration breaks v2-internals tests that assert the v2
bookmark layout. Added a test-only `JJF_DISABLE_V2_TO_V3_MIGRATION`
env var; set in `make_scratch_repo` (jjf-storage integration
tests) and `run_jjf` (jjf push/pull tests) so v2 coverage stays
alive until ticket 7's test sweep ports those assertions.

`cargo nextest run --workspace`: **458/458 green** (was 452 pre-
change; +6 new migration tests).

# Recommendation

Land. Branch `v3-migrator` on `~/p/jjforge-v3-migrator`; commit
`1541f4b0`. Closes c14e1c1. Unblocks tickets 5 (push/pull
refspec), 6 (cache port), 7 (test sweep). The
`JJF_DISABLE_V2_TO_V3_MIGRATION` env var is intentionally
test-only; ticket 7 deletes both the env-var checks and the
test helpers that set it.

# Confidence

High on the migration logic — the six tests cover the documented
acceptance criteria and reuse the proven v3 write-path primitives
(`hash_object` / `mktree` / `commit_tree` / `update_ref`). The
test opt-out is the only judgment call; it's consistent with how
the v2-shape integration tests handle the post-v3 `Storage::init`
contract (ticket `add0646`'s pattern).

# Open follow-ups

- Ticket 7 (test sweep) deletes the `JJF_DISABLE_V2_TO_V3_MIGRATION`
  env var alongside the v2-shape assertions.
- The v1→v2→v3 chained test asserts `>= 2` commits on the v3
  chain (not exact). The v1→v2 rename commit lacks a `Jjf-Op`
  trailer for the issue but DOES touch its path; whether to
  filter that out is a stylistic call ticket 7 can revisit.
- Empty issue trees (commits on the path filter with neither v1
  nor v2 file present) still produce v3 commits with empty
  trees; the trailer carries forward but the snapshot blob is
  absent. In practice this shouldn't occur for issues with
  well-formed v2 history; defensively handled.
